step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
**Clinical Reasoning:** Tocilizumab is a monoclonal antibody that targets the interleukin-6 (IL-6) receptor, not tumor necrosis factor (TNF)-alpha. It belongs to the class of interleukin-6 inhibitors, whereas TNF inhibitors specifically block TNF-alpha signaling. Therefore, tocilizumab does not act as a TNF inhibitor despite both being biologic disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs.
